item.title: Treasuries shrug at the ceasefire
item.description: Plus, tech sells bonds because they can

item.title: FirstFT: Hormuz reset to take ‘weeks’
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Russian shadow fleet and workplace AI bots

item.title: 商界女性
item.description: English Football boss Carol Shanahan says: you win or you learn. Plus, newly wealthy women need better advice; Nordic fertility measures are not working; menstrual leave; sudden hair loss; and the unstoppable rise of film executive Aparna Purohit

item.title: AI fuels private equity’s biggest ever windfalls
item.description: Bain Capital stands to pocket a $15bn gain on chipmaker Kioxia

item.title: Shadow fleet tanker seized by UK linked to Tate Modern sponsor
item.description: 17-year-old vessel was sold by shipping magnate George Economou’s TMS Group

item.title: UniCredit’s knack for clever deals proves unexpectedly costly
item.description: Andrea Orcel has taken an unhelpfully circuitous route to acquiring Commerzbank

item.title: The human brain is not a machine
item.description: This common comparison invites us to see ourselves as sub-optimal alternatives to AI agents

item.title: JPMorgan plans Chase expansion into Europe’s largest markets
item.description: US lender eyes France, Spain and Italy as it aims to grow digital bank

item.title: Claims of Palantir improvement in NHS driven by a handful of hospitals
item.description: Data shows some trusts have delivered fewer operations since adopting the US company’s technology

item.title: The darts business scores with the Luke Littler effect
item.description: A cottage industry run by family companies is attracting private equity as it expands globally

item.title: Europe’s AI champion Mistral vulnerable to Russian disinformation, study finds
item.description: Open-source generative models are worse at removing false news than others, according to Estonian researchers

item.title: US-Iran deal must be ‘material’ for Strait of Hormuz to reopen, tanker giant warns
item.description: Mitsui OSK Lines boss cautions that fleet owners need to build confidence after months of conflict

item.title: Private equity bosses warn of AI threat to bets on law and accountancy
item.description: Buyout groups that have invested heavily in professional services face disruption from developing technology

item.title: Europe’s Leading Patent Law Firms — 2026 ranking
item.description: Listings are based on recommendations made by clients and peers to FT research partner Statista

item.title: The price of risk is falling in a more dangerous world
item.description: Capital is pouring into insurance because of high returns and low volatility. But some professionals are worried about mispricing

item.title: Hedge funds bet against European carmakers on Chinese competition fears
item.description: Long-term debt and equity targeted as tens of billions of euros wiped off sector’s market value

item.title: HR must manage AI bots as well as humans, says Accenture executive
item.description: Matt Prebble says businesses will be forced to rethink leadership models

item.title: The reclusive heirs in line for billions when Europe’s top tankmaker lists
item.description: Unlikely cast of characters who own half of KNDS set for a windfall as Berlin prepares to buy in at an up to €20bn valuation

item.title: Newly wealthy women can be underserved by financial advisers
item.description: The asset management industry often fails to understand its female clients

item.title: ‘I will set the rules’: the rise of Aparna Purohit
item.description: The film executive is proof that being strong-minded pays

item.title: OpenAI spending hit $34bn last year ahead of planned IPO
item.description: Audited figures reveal mounting costs from model development, infrastructure and rapid expansion

item.title: How an AI windfall gave Bain Capital one of the most lucrative private equity deals ever
item.description: US firm stands to pocket profits of $15bn on 2018 buyout of Kioxia, the former Toshiba Memory

item.title: China’s economy shows signs of weakness in May
item.description: Retail sales decline for first time in more than three years and fixed asset investment slump deepens

item.title: Environment secretary casts doubt on £10bn Thames Water rescue
item.description: Emma Reynolds’ warning increases prospect of the nationalisation of Britain’s biggest water company

item.title: US billionaire owners of Crystal Palace explore sale
item.description: South London football club is enjoying its most successful ever period

item.title: ConocoPhillips set to sign deal with Syria to revive gas production
item.description: Agreement will be first by a US energy major with Damascus as it aims to rebuild activity after years of civil war

item.title: SpaceX shares gain for second day after blockbuster debut
item.description: Elon Musk’s AI, satellite and rocket group climbs 19.6% further on Monday

item.title: Chipmaker Nvidia seeks to raise over $25bn in first bond deal since 2021
item.description: Debt sale set to test investor appetite for further exposure to AI sector amid a deluge of borrowing

item.title: Murdochs’ aversion to big buyouts meets its match in the streaming age
item.description: Fox’s shares dip following news of $22bn acquisition of Roku

item.title: AI giants are learning a hard lesson about pricing power
item.description: Anthropic, until Friday’s White House move, looked like one of the more rationally valued companies in its peer group
